sections.heading: 🌍 Global Market Briefing — Asian Session
sections.content: Risk sentiment remains cautiously optimistic during Asian trading as markets digest overnight US strength and focus on emerging geopolitical developments. The Nikkei's explosive 3.14% rally leads global indices while USD strength persists against commodity currencies. Energy complex gains momentum with WTI approaching the psychological $100 level, reflecting supply-side concerns and seasonal demand patterns.

sections.heading: 📊 FOREX — Currency Strength
sections.content:
Strongest to Weakest: USD (+0.18% avg) > CHF > JPY > EUR > GBP > NZD > AUD (-0.56%)
Real money flows favor USD positioning as Treasury yield differentials widen. AUD weakness accelerates on China growth concerns and commodity rotation. JPY remains vulnerable despite marginal gains, with USD/JPY testing intervention zones near 159.00.

sections.heading: 💱 Key Currency Pairs
sections.content:
EUR/USD 1.1605 (-0.21%): Bearish momentum continues below 1.1620 resistance. ECB dovish pivot expectations weigh on single currency. Target 1.1580 support.
USD/JPY 159.0230 (+0.11%): Critical levels as pair flirts with 159.50 intervention threshold. BoJ verbal warnings intensify. Risk-reward favors fade on spikes.
AUD/USD 0.7115 (-0.56%): Sharp selloff breaks 0.7150 support on China PMI disappointment. Iron ore weakness compounds pressure. Next target 0.7080.

sections.heading: 💰 FX Flows & Positioning
sections.content:
• Corporate month-end USD demand accelerating across APAC corridors
• Commodity currency shorts building as China reopening trade unwinds
• JPY intervention risk premium rising with option skew tilting defensive
• EM FX facing dual pressure from dollar strength and risk-off positioning

sections.heading: 📈 EQUITIES — Indices
sections.content: Nikkei leads with explosive 3.14% gain to 61684 on tech sector rotation. US markets closed higher with Nasdaq outperforming at 26270 (+1.54%). European indices lag with DAX -0.30% reflecting automotive sector pressure. Asia-Pacific momentum driven by semiconductor rally and yen weakness benefits.

sections.heading: 🛢️ COMMODITIES
sections.content:
Gold $4516.10 (-0.42%): Consolidating below $4550 resistance as USD strength caps upside. Support holds at $4500.
WTI $99.69 (+1.46%): Approaching psychological $100 level on inventory draws and geopolitical premium. Bullish above $98.50.
Silver $75.31 (-1.14%): Underperforming gold with industrial demand concerns. Key support $74.00.
Natural Gas $3.039 (+1.17%): Weather-driven demand supporting near-term price action.

sections.heading: ₿ CRYPTO
sections.content:
BTC: $77582 (+0.14%) — Consolidating in upper range with resistance at $78000. Institutional accumulation continues near $76500 support.
ETH: $2127.41 (flat) — ETH/BTC ratio stabilizing at 0.027. Staking yield dynamics supporting base case.
XRP leads altcoins at $1.3730 (+0.56%) on regulatory clarity. SOL $86.49 (+0.52%) benefits from ecosystem growth.

sections.heading: 🎯 Intraday Trade Ideas
sections.content:
📈 LONG — USD/CAD above 1.3780 targeting 1.3820 | Nikkei futures on dips to 61500 | WTI crude momentum play above $100
📉 SHORT — AUD/USD below 0.7110 targeting 0.7080 | Gold fade rallies to $4530 | European equity indices on relief bounces

sections.heading: 📅 Today's Macro Drivers
sections.content: German IFO Business Climate (09:00 CET), US PCE deflator revision, China industrial profits data, 10Y Treasury yield monitoring above 4.35% threshold.
